notes: Standard suit for recovery of money. Fee calculated ad valorem on claimed amount. Section 7(i) of CFA.
Covered under Commercial Courts Act 2015. Specified value includes principal+interest+damages+penalty. Fee same as regular suit.
Section 7 of Family Courts Act read with Schedule II. Fixed fee ₹150 in most states.
Criminal application. Fixed fee ₹100 in most states. Schedule II Article 1.
Constitutional remedy under Article 226/227. Fixed fee ₹500 in most states.
Valued on plaintiff's share of property. Section 7(iv)(b) of CFA.
Valued on agreement/consideration value. Section 7(x) of CFA.
Valued on market value of property/right. Section 7(iv)(c) of CFA.
Valued on plaintiff's estimation. Section 7(iv)(d) of CFA.
Valued on market value of property. Section 7(v) of CFA.
Under Arbitration Act 1996. Fixed fee ₹650 under Schedule II.
Appeal from commercial court. Same fee as original suit.
For execution of decree. Valued on decree amount. Section 7(xii) of CFA.
Probate fee typically 2.5% of estate value. Section 19-H of CFA.
Fee typically 2.5% of debt value. Part X of CFA.
Valued on annual rent. Section 7(xi)(cc) of CFA.
Fee under Consumer Protection Act 2019. Separate slab structure.
Under Companies Act 2013. Fixed fee varies by petition type.
